mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, equity discount rates and recession fear both ease, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because rate cuts then read as liquidity support rather than a growth panic response.
failure_condition: Reject if falling FEDFUNDS does not precede positive 20d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above the prior three-month level signals deteriorating household income and weaker demand, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should appear after the unemployment rate crosses a meaningful threshold, not during flat labor markets.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment rises are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases or if the signal has no edge after threshold crossings.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand is improving across consumption and housing, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is stronger when both series accelerate together rather than one offsetting the other.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS and HOUST strengthening fails to improve 60d SPX returns versus baseline, or if either series alone explains the effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while PAYEMS is not, firms are producing more output per worker, which supports margins and positive near-term equity returns. The signal fails if output growth is accompanied by a broad labor-market deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO with flat or modest PAYEMS do not produce above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if labor weakness dominates the response.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% tightens financial conditions and compresses valuation multiples, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is still rising, because real rates remain restrictive and policy cannot be assumed to ease quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if high-fed-funds episodes do not show negative 5d SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the signal vanishes when inflation is elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Steady PAYEMS growth without a corresponding jump in UNRATE signals resilient payroll demand and low recession risk, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is invalid if payroll gains come with rising unemployment, which would indicate labor churn rather than strength.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll expansion paired with stable or falling unemployment does not predict positive 20d SPX returns, or if the effect reverses when UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in GDPC1 relative to the prior quarter signals slowing aggregate demand and weaker corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal should be most informative when the GDP contraction is confirmed rather than a one-off noisy print.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 declines do not precede below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the directional edge is absent in confirmed slowdown periods.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST remains depressed, industrial output is improving faster than construction activity, implying a supply-side rebound that can lift earnings expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should fail when housing weakness is too severe, because that would indicate broader cyclical fragility rather than a healthy rebound.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO with weak HOUST does not produce positive 20d SPX returns, or if housing weakness overwhelms the industrial improvement.
